what_it_does: A posted channel note in which Tim comments on another noticeboard item, agreeing that gesture interactions can be understood as typed graph/tree-walking operations.
observations: YAML frontmatter identifies the item as a note with id item-e25edb34, source claude_code, state posted, author_session tim, channel dragnet-development
It links to board://item-e62c2915 with kind commented_on
The body quotes a prior statement describing gestures as pure tree-walking (tap, thumb-up/down, left/right, release) and states agreement
standouts: The assertion that 'the data model is the algorithm; the gesture is a cursor over it'
The mapping of physical gestures to typed graph edges: part_of parent/child and siblings
The emphasis on separating document knowledge from gesture mechanics
conventions: Markdown file with YAML frontmatter metadata
Uses board:// URI addressing and a commented_on link kind
History records a single filed transition from null to posted by tim
notes: Tim's note endorsing the framing of gesture input as a type-aware tree-walking cursor that is independent of document semantics.
questions: What is the full content and context of the referenced item-e62c2915?
What typed relationships beyond part_of are intended in the graph?
How does the 'release → commit' step interact with the cursor state?
purpose_vs_actual: The file is titled and structured as a point/comment note; it records agreement with a design principle, matching its stated purpose.
apparent_themes: Separation of concerns between interaction gesture and data model
Graph/tree traversal as the core navigation mechanism
Typed directional relationships such as part_of and siblings
intention_signals: Reinforce the idea that gesture logic should be a generic, type-aware walker over a graph and that document semantics should live entirely in the data model.
novelty: The explicit re-framing of multi-touch gestures as a type-aware tree-walking cursor rather than document-aware commands.
contribution: Captures a design principle stating that gesture input should act as a neutral cursor over typed graph edges while the data model owns structure and relationships.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim in dragnet-development commenting on a prior item and agreeing that gesture input should be modeled as a typed graph-walking cursor separate from document knowledge.
intention_for_embedding: To record agreement that gesture interactions are best understood as walking typed edges in a tree or graph rather than as document-aware commands.
